title: Render — The cloud for builders
description: Deploy and scale any app or agent from your first user to your billionth. Build faster on intuitive cloud infrastructure for the modern web.

sections.title: The cloud for builders.
sections.lede: Deploy and scale any app or agent from your first user to your billionth. Build faster on intuitive cloud infrastructure for the modern web.

sections.text: Forge-static approximation of render.com authored from SSR-extractable headings + meta description. Pixel-rep rotation target per PlausiDen-Forge #222. Not affiliated with Render; the substrate uses Render as a reference shape for the editorial_purity_gate's deploy-platform audit.

sections.body: Click, click, done. Select a service. Deploy your code. Render does the rest.

items.hint: Auto-scaling, zero-downtime deploys from git push. Free TLS. The default shape for new apps.
Long-running tasks, scheduled jobs, queue consumers. Same deploy + observability surface as web services.
Daily backups, point-in-time restore, automatic failover. Connection pooling baked in.
In-memory cache + queue substrate. Cluster mode for horizontal scaling.
Service mesh between your apps + databases without operator-side VPC config. Default-deny posture.

sections.text: The reference shape: a deploy platform where the operator-facing flow is three explicit steps (select / deploy / receive), not a 12-tab dashboard. Render's substrate commits to keeping it that way.

sections.title: Open the live Render site.
sections.lede: This is the Forge-static approximation. The actual product lives at the canonical URL.
